title: argyroxiphium sandwicense subsp. macrocephalum
intro: Argyroxiphium sandwicense subsp. macrocephalum, also known as the Ma'o hau hele or yellow hibiscus, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant that is native to the island of Hawaii. Traditionally, the plant's flowers, leaves, and stems have been used for medicinal purposes, with properties that include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds. The health benefits of Argyroxiphium sandwicense subsp. macrocephalum include the potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of colds and fever, as well as its traditional use as a natural remedy for skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and salves, which are often used in traditional Hawaiian medicine.
